Who REALLY defined pure goalscoring ruthlessness in the early 2000s Premier League?

Thierry Henry vs Ruud van Nistelrooy. Henry was pure artistry: explosive pace, elegant finishes, scoring from anywhere. But RVN? A cold, clinical predator. 95 goals in 150 PL games, often one touch, one goal. Was Henry the better player, or was Ruud simply the deadlier striker in the box? Debate it. #PLHistory #Arsenal #ManUtd

Think modern defensive football is boring?

Helenio Herrera's Inter Milan took it to another level with Catenaccio. A dedicated sweeper, fierce man-marking, and counters that were pure murder. They weren't just winning; they were dominating by suffocating opponents into submission. Pure genius, absolute villainy.
